name: PM-KISAN (Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi)
description: An initiative by the Government of India that provides an assured income support of ₹6,000 per year to all landholding farmer families across the country.
benefits: Financial assistance of ₹6,000 per year paid in three equal installments of ₹2,000 every four months.
Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) straight into the farmer's linked Aadhaar-enabled bank account.
100% funding support from the Central Government of India.
eligibility: All landholding farmer families holding cultivable agricultural land in their name.
Must not belong to institutional ownership or high-income/taxpayer categories.
Must have a valid Aadhaar card and linked active bank account.

name: PMFBY (Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana)
description: A comprehensive crop insurance scheme to provide financial support to farmers experiencing crop loss or damage arising out of natural calamities, pests, & diseases.
benefits: Comprehensive risk insurance covers yield losses from pre-sowing to post-harvest.
Very low premium rates for farmers: 2% for Kharif crops, 1.5% for Rabi crops, and 5% for annual commercial/horticultural crops.
Fast online claim settlement based on satellite remote sensing data and drone crop cutting.
eligibility: All farmers including sharecroppers and tenant farmers growing notified crops in notified areas are eligible.
Highly recommended for farmers taking crop loans (KCC) from formal banking channels.

name: PM-KUSUM (Solar Pump Scheme)
description: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Urja Suraksha evam Utthaan Mahabhiyan ensures clean energy for farming by installing standalone solar agriculture pumps and solarizing grid-connected pumps.
benefits: Provides off-grid solar agricultural pumps up to 7.5 HP capacity.
Saves high electricity bills and guarantees stable power during the daytime for irrigation.
Allows selling surplus solar power back to the government grid for additional income.
eligibility: Individual farmers, water user associations, co-operatives, and community clusters.
Must have a water source and cultivable land requiring irrigation pump upgrade.

name: PMKSY (Per Drop More Crop - Drip Irrigation Subsidy)
description: Promotes micro-irrigation systems (drip and sprinkler) to maximize water use efficiency, reduce fertilizer runoff, and increase overall crop productivity.
benefits: Subsidy covering a major portion of micro-irrigation system installation costs.
Water saving up to 40% to 50% compared to traditional flood irrigation methods.
Substantially reduces weed growth, soil erosion, and labor costs for watering.
eligibility: Small and marginal farmers receive up to 55% subsidy assistance.
Other category farmers receive up to 45% subsidy assistance.
Minimum land requirement of 0.2 hectares with access to borewell or water storage tank.
